messages.message: My dear Austria, I admire your position in the Balkans and your strategic depth. I propose we remain neutral toward each other so we can focus on countering the ambitions of Russia and Germany. No need to compete over the Italian coast—I have little interest in expansion beyond my own borders.

France, I must say I appreciate your strength along the Mediterranean. Perhaps we could maintain peaceful relations, allowing both of us to grow unchallenged in our respective regions. A quiet alliance might serve us both well.

Hi France,
Just reaching out to establish communications. Would you like to DMZ Bur? Hopefully we can both grow and thrive while staying out of each other's way, let me know what you're thinking in terms of alliances and future plans and I'll see if I can get on board!

Hey Russia!
So what are your thoughts for the first few turns of the game? I reckon we could probably work something out to get a bit of an advantage if we communicate well!
Turkey, I hope our paths remain untroubled. I have no interest in your lands or the Aegean—I respect your power and would prefer to avoid any conflict. Let us keep the waters calm between us.
England! How's it going?
Sounds good Italy, perhaps we can work together in the future.
France! How's it going?
Germany! How's it going?
Austria! How's it going?

Hey Germany! Absolutely, I like the idea of a DMZ in Bur. I think there are a bunch of ways our two nations could cooperate, but I definitely understand your hesitation to commit to anything too early. Have you heard from England yet? If he seems like he could be a bit... aggressive, I think we should definitely be on the lookout for a potential western triple.

If that doesn't sound like something you'd be down for, I'm happy to work with you either way. Let me know what you think!
Hey Turkey! It's going well here, how's the east treating you so far?
Hi Italy, just establishing communications, hope things go well for you. Let me know if there's anything I can help you with and I'll see what I can do!
So far so good! But who knows how long it will last haha. I do appreciate it though :)
Hi Austria, just establishing communications, hope things go well for you. Let me know if there's anything I can help you with and I'll see what I can do!
Hi Turkey, just establishing communications, hope things go well for you. Let me know if there's anything I can help you with and I'll see what I can do!
Well if you need anything, let me know!

My dear Turkey, I appreciate your response. Let us indeed keep our mutual interests aligned for now. The Aegean remains a calm sea under your wise rule, and I shall not trouble your peace. Perhaps one day we might find ways to assist each other, but for now, I wish you continued strength.

Hi Russia, I'll be straightforward and ask that we DMZ Silesia and Prussia. If there's anything I can do to help you with diplomacy or game mechanics, let me know. Good luck and let me know if you'd like to collaborate in any way!
And the same goes for you!
And the same to you!
What are your plans for the opening turn, do you think I can be of any assistance?
Hi England, just establishing communications, hope things go well for you. Let me know if there's anything I can help you with and I'll see what I can do!
That sounds great! Let me know if there's anything I can do for you as well :)

My dear Italy! I'm very happy with your proposal. I'd be happy to keep Tyr, Ven, and Tri as Dmzs if you are.

Good luck this game!

Hey Italy! You're welcome, I'm glad you appreciate it. I agree, the ability for us to maintain peace would be a benefit to us both. I'd like to be friendly, if that works for you. We could demilitarize Piedmont, Gol, West Med, and North Africa, and focus on our respective spheres. What do you think?

I'd be fine with a Dmz in Sil and Pru.
Can I convince you to let me into Swe? Obviously it's not ideal to be bounced out.
Also, would you be ok with opening to Hol rather than Den? That way I can count on not being bounced out.
Hi Turkey! It's going well. How about yourself?

Germany, a most welcome message. I admire your power and the strength of your position. I propose we maintain peaceful relations as well—there is certainly enough space for both of us to grow without conflict. Should the need arise for cooperation, I shall not hesitate to reach out.
Hi Russia! I'm looking forward to a great game with you. Would you like to keep Galicia a DMZ? I'd be happy to work with you against Turkey or Germany.

Hi Germany! The same to you.

As Germany, you're really the only power that can help me, so I'll make sure to let you know if I need anything. I'll do the same for you!
Hello Turkey! I'd be interested in working together. What are your thoughts on a Black Sea bounce?

My esteemed Austria, I hope your plans proceed smoothly. I remain committed to our mutual understanding of non-aggression. Perhaps we might consider future cooperation in the Balkans, should the need arise.
Hi England! It's great to hear from you. I know we don't have much direct cooperation to do at this point, but I'll do what I can to make sure we both do well. Good luck this game!
Hi France! It's great to hear from you. I know we don't have much direct cooperation to do at this point, but I'll do what I can to make sure we both do well. Good luck this game!

My dear Turkey, I appreciate your offer of assistance, though I must confess my ambitions are modest at this stage—I aim only to consolidate my own holdings and maintain peace along our borders. Perhaps in the future, when the map has shifted and new opportunities arise, we may find ways to cooperate. For now, I shall keep the Aegean calm under your watchful eye.
Hey Austria! I completely agree, the prospect of us cooperating is exciting! And I hope you do well, too. I'll let you know if I hear anything interesting from your neighbors.
I look forward to cooperation in the Balkans. I believe that our nations working together can accomplish great things.

My esteemed Austria, your offer to keep Tyr, Ven, and Tri as DMZs is most generous. I appreciate your gesture and will respect this arrangement for now. Perhaps in the future, when the balance of power shifts, we might explore more direct cooperation. Until then, let us remain peaceful neighbors.

Glad to hear it! I'll be sure to do the same for you.

Have you had any contact with Germany or Italy?
Hey Germany! I think our nations are strong together and have a lot of great options if we work together. Do you have any early thoughts on who you want to target?
Wonderful! I'm glad that we're on the same page.
Hey Austria! You too! I agree, it will be some time until our borders touch, but I would love to hear what your early thoughts are on this game. Hopefully we can work together soon.
Hey France! Do you have any interest in a strong alliance?
Hello Austria! I'm very much looking forward to the game as well.

Hey England! Definitely, I'd like that. I'm not sure what my long-term plans are, but I'd be open to a western triple if that would be a good idea for you. I could take the med, and you and Germany would take the center and north. What do you think?

My good friend France, I admire your vision of peaceful cooperation. Demilitarizing regions such as Piedmont and the West Med sounds agreeable, though I must remain cautious with my own borders. I see no reason to oppose your idea at this time—we shall grow in harmony, each in our own sphere.
My early thoughts are to try and maintain peaceful borders with my neighbors and see how this game plays out. You?
Hey Russia! I think our nations can have a great relationship and there are many ways we can work together in the north to our mutual benefit. What are your early plans for this game?
Would you like to keep Galicia a DMZ?

My dear France, I agree wholeheartedly with your vision of peaceful cooperation. Demilitarizing Piedmont, Gol, West Med, and North Africa sounds most agreeable, though I must remain cautious regarding my own security. I propose we each maintain a light presence along contested borders to ensure mutual confidence. Let us grow in harmony, each in our own sphere.
Hey Turkey! Pretty well, how about you? There isn't much we can do now, but let's keep the line open. Hopefully we can work together in the future!
That would be an interesting alliance. What would you think about a strong DMZ in the Channel as well?

My esteemed Austria, I am most pleased with your enthusiasm for Balkan cooperation. Should the need arise for coordination or shared interests, I shall not hesitate to reach out. For now, let us remain peaceful neighbors and watch the balance of power shift to our advantage.
Sounds good to me!
I've been in touch with them, not sure what to expect from them yet though.
Pretty good so far! What are your thoughts on the opening move?
Hey Russia, how are you doing?
Sounds good to me! Hopefully we'll meet up in the future
That sounds good. The more people we can get to ally with us, the better. We can try to get information on our neighbors and see if they are allied with each other.
I agree!
I don't know either. Italy is either playing it pretty close to the chest, or just not very experienced. Germany hasn't said much yet either.
I was wondering what you think about Russia and Austria?

I'd be very much interested in an agreement. What would you be comfortable with?
I'm happy to agree to not move an army to Stp in exchange for you taking Nor with a fleet instead of an army.
I'll probably try to grab Swe, unless there's something else you would like from me.

Germany, I admire your strength and vision. I believe peaceful coexistence between our nations serves both our ambitions. Perhaps one day we shall find a reason to collaborate, but for now, let us keep the Italian coast undisturbed.
I agree to a triple alliance if that's the case!
Hello France! I'm doing pretty well. How are you doing?
I appreciate it, I hope we will both have the opportunity to cooperate in the future!
I couldn't agree more. Good luck this spring!
I couldn't agree more!
I'm going to be going for the Balkans, but I haven't worked out any specifics yet. How about yourself?
That all sounds good to me. I agree to that. We can probably also coordinate against Germany if the need arises.
Great! Let me know if I can be of any assistance!
Hey England! I'm open to the idea, but I'm going to need to know for sure if Germany is in. I'd like to be able to make a move on Italy if he's not part of it. A DMZ in the channel sounds good to me.
I'd be comfortable with that.
I'm alright, I'm trying to get a feel for my neighbors. So far, England seems friendly, but he hasn't told me anything about his plans beyond that. How are you getting on with your neighbors?
Have you spoken to Russia at all? He sent me a one-line greeting, but I don't have a clue what his intentions are at this point.
They both seem to be cordial to me. I think we have come to an agreement about Scandanavia, which is good.
I couldn't agree more!
I'm still trying to decide what to do. I was thinking about going for Bulgaria, but not sure what else
Wonderful!

My dear Turkey, I share your hope for future cooperation—it is wise to keep the Aegean calm for now. As you watch the Balkans and I watch the Italian coast, let us each grow in strength and patience. The map may yet bring us together when the time is right.
Have you talked with Russia yet?
I will certainly do that! How are things going with Russia?
I haven't heard anything from him about his intentions yet. He's been pretty short with me.
Also, have you heard anything from Russia yet?
That sounds good to me.
I have, but nothing too specific has come up
Yeah that sounds about right.

My esteemed Austria, I appreciate your kind wishes. Let us continue to maintain peaceful relations as we each secure our own borders. Perhaps the Balkans will soon present opportunities for quiet coordination—should the moment arise, I shall be ready.
That sounds wonderful, I look forward to the opportunity
Hey Russia, are you going for Rumania first turn?
That is the plan. I'm still figuring out what to do with the rest of my units.
Yeah, I think you're right.
Yeah, he's been pretty short with me. If he's the same with you then I'd be worried about an R/T.
They seem good, he seems interested in not attacking me in the north.
Let me know. I have not heard anything from Germany.
I'd be open to discussing the details of what would be a "direct" cooperation.
Ok, good to know. I'll keep an eye on him.
Alright, I haven't heard anything from him either. We'll see what he says.
Gotcha, sounds good
I'll have to be careful. Thanks for the heads up
Have you heard anything from Germany?
I haven't heard anything from Germany.
You're welcome.
Well that's no good.
I think he's just the quiet type.
I haven't seen many Germany's not be cordial in my experience.
That's fair. I think he probably just hasn't been on yet.

My good friend France, I admire your spirit of cooperation. Let us ensure that peaceful growth remains our mutual priority. I remain open to future coordination, particularly in the West Med, where our interests align most closely.
Yeah, that's probably fair.
I completely agree. Have you heard anything from our German neighbor? I haven't heard from him yet, and I wanted to check in and see what you think.
Germany, I appreciate your strength and vision. Let us remain peaceful neighbors for now—I see no reason to trouble the Italian coast when both our ambitions may flourish unchallenged.
I think he's just quiet.
Yeah, you might be right.
Have you heard anything from Germany or Italy?

I think Italy's just being cautious, and he hasn't told me anything beyond "peaceful cooperation" yet. Germany has also sent me a short message, but I haven't heard anything from him beyond a standard DMZ in Burgundy. Have you heard from him yet?

My dear France, I have yet to hear any firm stance from Germany, though I suspect they prefer quiet stability along their southern border. I believe we both stand to benefit from a united front in the West Med—perhaps we might consider coordinating further if the opportunity arises.
Hey Italy! Just wanted to check in and get an idea of how things are shaping up for you.
My dear Turkey, I am pleased you look forward to future cooperation. Let us ensure the Aegean remains tranquil for now—I shall watch the map closely and remain ready when opportunity knocks.

My esteemed Austria, I appreciate your openness to direct cooperation. While I see great strategic value in such an arrangement, particularly in the Balkans, I must remain cautious and focus on securing my own borders for now. Perhaps we could discuss shared interests in Tyr, Ven, or Tri at a later stage when the map allows for such coordination.
I'm happy to discuss them at any time. We should stay open to changing our stance as things progress.

Dear France, I have yet to receive a firm response from Germany, though I suspect they prefer quiet stability along their southern border. I remain open to further coordination with you in the West Med—perhaps we might consider a mutual defense or quiet expansion strategy should the opportunity arise.
Hello Italy, how are you doing?
What are your plans for the game?

My dear England, I must say I admire your strength and position along the North Sea. I aim only to consolidate my own holdings and maintain peace along the Mediterranean coast. I see no reason why we cannot grow in harmony, each in our own sphere.

My dear England, I find your kind inquiry most agreeable. I remain focused on consolidating my modest holdings along the Mediterranean coast—I have little interest in grand ambitions at this stage. Let us grow untroubled by one another, each in our own sphere.

My esteemed Austria, I appreciate your openness to evolving stances. I remain committed to peaceful relations between our nations and see great value in maintaining stability along our shared border. Should the need arise for quiet cooperation in the Balkans, I shall be most receptive.
I'm glad to hear that you're open to that, but yes, I think that's a good idea. I could occupy the north and you the south.
I have now. I get the same impression.

My dear Russia, I hope your ambitions proceed smoothly. I am but a humble power seeking only to preserve peace along my borders. I see no need for conflict between us—for now, let us each pursue our own interests unchallenged.
Cool. I think we're both fairly experienced players as well, so it's definitely interesting.

My esteemed Austria, I appreciate your openness to evolving stances. I remain committed to peaceful relations between our nations and see great value in maintaining stability along our shared border. Should the need arise for quiet cooperation in the Balkans, I shall be most receptive.

My dear Russia, I hope your ambitions proceed smoothly. I am but a humble power seeking only to preserve peace along my borders. I see no need for conflict between us—for now, let us each pursue our own interests unchallenged.

My good friend France, I am delighted to hear your suggestion of division—let you occupy the north and I the south. A quiet and harmonious Mediterranean seems most agreeable. I shall respect your northern ambitions while I consolidate my own southern sphere.
Sounds good to me. Good luck in your opening moves!
Sounds good to me!
That sounds good to me. Best of luck to you!
I'm thinking Bulgaria, and Greece.
That's probably wise. How's your relationship with Austria?
Hi, I haven't heard from Austria yet but Russia seems like he might be amiable. Haven't spoken to him too much to be honest
Italy, I'm glad to hear we can be on the same page, let's keep in touch.
Sounds like a plan!
Hey Russia, I'm fine with letting you into Swe as long as you're looking to fight England. What's your situation looking like down south?
Not really, still waiting to hear from France and Russia so I'll let you know if there's anything I think you should do. I'm assuming you'll be looking for Norway?

Hi France, Russia seems amiable but he's pretty tight lipped. England also seems friendly but I'll have to see what his moves are before I can confirm anything. Would you be looking to take on England or something else?

I'm not sure at this point, but I think either would be fine with me. I think either option could work for us, depending on what our neighbors are doing. Have you heard anything from Italy or Austria?
Yeah, they seem to be friendly for the time being. Austria also reached out to me, haven't heard from Italy
Hi Italy, anything going on down south? I haven't heard anything from Austria if that's of concern
Cool, sounds good. I guess we'll just have to see what happens in the opening moves and go from there.
That's the plan. How are your relations with France? Have you spoken about Belgium?
Yup
We've Dmz'd Burgundy. I'm open to a western triple if you're keen
My dear Russia, I appreciate your kind words. Indeed, peace along our borders allows both of us to focus on our true ambitions elsewhere. I wish you continued success in your northern campaigns.

Germany, I appreciate your concern for Austria's affairs—indeed, the southern front remains quiet and stable under mutual respect. I see no threat from Austria at present, and I hope your own southern flank remains undisturbed. Let us continue to grow untroubled.
And to you as well!
That's what I heard from him. It sounds good to me!
Thank you for your transparency, much appreciated. Good luck in the south!
What are your plans for the Balkans? It'd be great if we can cooperate
I'd rather you take Belgium and Norway and I take Holland and Denmark. I have no problem with you taking Sweden as well
Sounds good! I like the idea of us working together and I will focus on the north and you the center and south.
Cool
Great!
So France seems like he is down with the triple as well.
Good to hear, hopefully the three of us can work together and flourish!
Hey France, I've spoken with England and we're looking to keep our options open as we're still waiting on Austria to respond to me. Have you talked to him at all?
Ok, cheers for the heads up
Have you been talking with France?
Not sure, we haven't really had too much interaction yet
Alright. Well I look forward to working with you.
Thanks, same to you
Yes, I think we will have a strong start!
Not about Austria, no. I was just wondering if you had heard anything. What did England have to say about Austria?
Nope, not a peep from him
Nothing at all
Ok. Well I guess that's good for us.
I agree, hopefully we can get him on board with a western triple
No worries
Hey Germany, what are your thoughts about Russia?
How are things going down south?
It looks like Austria is working with Russia and Turkey. I don't think Russia is going to have much trouble holding on to Sevastopol though.
Pretty good so far! I'm not entirely sure where everyone's loyalties lie. How's it looking for you?
It seems you are quite committed in the north!
Hey Russia! It looks like Austria and Turkey are working together. I'd suggest trying to get Italy on your side. If the two of you could work together, you could easily take Turkey down.
That's not what it looks like to me either, but it's good to be safe!
Yep!
What do you have in mind? I'll be focused on getting my neutrals but if you have a plan I'd be glad to weigh in
Hey Austria, any chance you could use some help with Russia?
Indeed it does. I'll have to get more into the South and leave you to deal with France.
Thanks for the advice. I'll see what I can do.
Fair enough!
What are your plans for this next move?
Yeah, I can't really help Austria so it's his call if he wants Russia to get the extra center or not
Also, I'm assuming you'll be taking Belgium?
I guess this move just sealed the fact that we're working together haha
Are you planning on taking Sweden this year?
Have you talked to Austria yet?

That's my plan. I was hoping that England would move to Norwegian sea and then support my fleet to Norway next year.
Has England said anything to you about this?
A pretty standard Austria/turkey opening. So, what now?

Hey England, Russia just messaged me about supporting him into Norway next turn. I don't know if he's trying to trick me or what, but he wants me to support him into Sweden. Have you had any communication with him yet?
How are things going for you so far?

My esteemed Austria, I appreciate Germany’s mention of our shared border. Rest assured, Italy intends to remain peaceful and focused inward for now. Perhaps one day we may find cause for quiet cooperation, but for now, let us preserve stability.

Yes, I would appreciate your help. I had agreed to keep Galicia a DMZ and he broke it on the first turn. I'm not sure if he's moving against you, but if you want to go for Warsaw and Sweden, I won't complain.
I couldn't agree more. Good luck this fall.
Russia broke our agreement to keep Galicia a DMZ, so I think I need to deal with that. You?
That sounds good to me. I'll hold in Denmark. Have you spoken to England at all?
So much for that DMZ...
Yeah, I'm sorry about that. I hope there are no hard feelings.
Oh nevermind, turns out he wants me to bounce you out of Denmark

England has said that he will be focusing on France. I'm not sure if that is still true or not.
I appreciate your support into Swe.
Ah, thanks for the heads up
I'm still trying to figure out what I'm going to do next
Hey Turkey! I'm alright, Germany and England seem to be friendly, but I don't know how long that will last. What about you?
Yeah, it's tough. If you and Russia are set on fighting me, I don't think you'll have much luck. If not, I'm happy to work with you against Russia.
Not sure yet, but it's looking pretty alright
I'm open to working with you. What do you suggest?
No hard feelings at all. I look forward to a long and fruitful relationship.
Cool, thanks for the info.
Yeah, I'll be taking Belgium. And yeah, I don't blame Austria. Hopefully Russia won't get too crippled.
It depends on what we think Russia is going to do. He's likely going to use Sev to support an attack into Rumania, so you could probably get into the Black Sea this turn.
Yeah that's what I'm thinking
Hey Italy! What are your plans for the next turn?
Hey Germany, I was wondering what you're thinking about Sweden?
Agreed! Best of luck!
Welcome!
Yeah I don't blame you, this opening was a pretty standard one.
Yeah, France and I seem to be on the same page. He seems pretty reasonable

My good friend France, I have received Germany’s greetings and reassurances. I remain confident that peace along our borders will allow both of us to grow unchallenged. Perhaps we might discuss future coordination in the West Med should the opportunity arise.
How's the situation looking down south?
Alright, sounds good
I'm still trying to figure that out, what do you think we should do?

Dear Turkey, I appreciate your inquiry! Italy’s plans remain focused on domestic stability and quiet consolidation along our southern borders. We have no interest in the Aegean or Balkans at this time—those are better left to more ambitious powers. Should the need arise, I would be delighted to discuss Mediterranean cooperation at a more convenient season.
Same to you!
Yeah, we will see how this plays out though haha
Not to sure what he's doing in the south. Not a good sign for sure.
I was thinking I'd let him in
Let's do it.
No worries, I think he'll be able to get into Sweden
Thank you for your response, it is appreciated!
Sounds good
You should probably be prepared in case England tries to stab. I'll try to figure out what his plans are and pass it along
Definitely. I look forward to it.
Austria/turkey are working together, and it doesn't look like I can make any progress. Italy is pretty uncommitted.
Alright
Hi Italy, how's the situation looking?
Thanks for the warning

My dear Austria, thank you for your kind words. I share your sentiment fully—stability is always preferable to conflict. Perhaps in the coming years we might find mutual benefit in the Balkans, though for now, Italy intends to remain a peaceful neighbor.
But what's the deal with you and Austria? Is that an alliance?

My good France, I hear your voice carries far in the West Med. Rest assured, Italy’s ambitions lie inland, not across the sea. Let us continue to enjoy our mutual understanding while others stir trouble elsewhere.
Yeah, Italy is being very odd. I'm trying to probe Austria and Turkey a bit more to see what they're up to
Of course. I hope this helps.

My dear Turkey, your kind words are always welcome. I admire your strength and vision in the region. Rest assured, Italy's focus remains on stability and peace in the south. If the time comes when our paths may align in mutual interest, I will be ready with open arms and clear intentions.
I couldn't agree more.
Alright, that would be great.
Likewise, Turkey would be delighted to assist you in any way it can
Let's hope so.
Not necessarily, we're just being friendly

My esteemed France, your confidence is heartening. Let us continue to ensure peace along our borders while others stir unrest elsewhere. Should the need arise for quiet cooperation in the West Med, I remain at your disposal.
How's your relationship with Russia?

Good day, Germany. All remains calm along our borders, and Italy continues to focus on internal consolidation. I admire your strength and vision—I would be delighted to discuss the future of southern Europe should opportunity arise.
Any word from Italy?

My dear Austria, your steadfast commitment to stability is most reassuring. I admire your strength in the East, and I hope we may one day find common cause in the Balkans. Until then, let us preserve peace and mutual respect.
I'd be up for another bounce.
He said all is quiet on his southern front and he wants to maintain peace with me. Seems a bit odd

My esteemed Turkey, your kind words are always welcome. I admire your power and vision in the region. Should the time come when our paths may align in mutual interest, I remain ready with open arms and clear intentions.
Pretty good I think. Do you have any sway over him?
Glad to hear it. Let me know what I can do for you as well.
I see that Austria and Turkey are working together. If you and I are going to work together, I think we should coordinate our attacks.
I'd say so
What are you thinking for your next move?

My good France, I share your hope for peace along our borders. Let us continue to enjoy our mutual understanding while others stir trouble elsewhere. Should the West Med ever require coordination, I remain at your disposal.
Sounds good to me.
Sounds good to me
I get the sense he's trying to find someone to turn on in the West so he can focus on the East.
Probably Denmark
Wonderful, it is good to hear!
I'm not sure I can do much for you right now. I'd like to maintain good relations with you in the North so I can focus on the south.
I'd be more worried about France than Russia. Russia seems on our side.
Gotcha
Haha, I see what you did there.
I'd also like to bounce again in the Black Sea.
I'm sure you can understand why I chose not to include that in my response.

My good France, I take your remarks in the spirit they were intended—playful and diplomatic. Let us not dwell on the past, but look to the future. If peace along our borders remains your aim, then surely we both benefit from quiet cooperation in the West Med.
Great to hear
What are you thinking?

My esteemed Turkey, your warm words are always a pleasure to receive. I admire your strength in the East and remain confident that peace along our borders will allow both our nations to grow unchallenged. Should the time come for Mediterranean cooperation, I remain at your disposal.
Makes sense. Good luck in the south!
Yeah

My dear Austria, I appreciate your continued commitment to stability and mutual respect. While Italy remains focused on internal consolidation, I remain open to future cooperation, especially in the Balkans, should opportunity arise.
Still not entirely sure. I'm probably going to play it safe this turn, and just get my one build

My dear France, I must say your remarks are ever so entertaining. Perhaps I did leave a bit of ambiguity in my letters—after all, diplomacy is an art best left to interpretation. Let us hope our mutual understanding remains firm, even if our words do not always align perfectly.

My esteemed Austria, I hope peace continues to reign along our shared borders. I remain ever respectful of your strength and vision in the East. Perhaps one day soon, we may discuss quiet cooperation in the Balkans—should the opportunity arise.
Haha, I love it. I don't know how else to put it.
Okay, sounds like a plan
Agreed
Germany, your strength is admirable. I remain open to future discussions about southern Europe’s future, should the time come when our interests align. For now, let us focus on stability and growth.
Likewise!
Thanks, you too!

My dear France, I must say your sense of humor is as charming as ever. Let us not dwell on the playful ambiguity of words—after all, diplomacy is best left to the artful dance of mutual understanding. I remain confident that peace along our borders will allow both our nations to grow unchallenged. Should the West Med ever require quiet cooperation, I remain at your disposal.
I think I'm just going to try to hold my own in the south. Maybe a move to Galicia.
Alright, sounds like a plan
Great
Alright, my good friend Italy, you've said enough.

My esteemed Turkey, your kind words are always a pleasure to receive. I admire your strength and vision in the East, and I remain confident that peace along our borders will allow both our nations to grow unchallenged. Should the time come for Mediterranean cooperation, I remain at your disposal.

Germany, I appreciate your warm response. I remain open to future discussions about southern Europe’s future, should the time come when our interests align. For now, let us preserve stability and mutual respect.
It looks like you and France will make quick work of Italy.
On second thought, I think I'll hold.

My dear France, I must say your laughter is as warm as your diplomacy is... inconsistent. Let us not waste time on jests—after all, we both have greater ambitions to pursue. I remain confident that peace along our borders will allow both of us to grow unchallenged. Should the West Med ever require coordination, I remain at your disposal.
Okay, let me know if you change your mind
Agreed, good luck to you

My esteemed Turkey, your warm response is most appreciated. I remain confident that peace along our borders will allow both our nations to grow unchallenged. Should the time come for Mediterranean cooperation, I remain at your disposal. Let us preserve stability for now.
I look forward to it. I look forward to a long and prosperous relationship.
Will do

My dear Austria, I hope peace continues to reign along our shared borders. I remain ever respectful of your strength and vision in the East. Perhaps soon we may discuss quiet cooperation in the Balkans—should opportunity arise.

Germany, your strength is admirable. I remain open to future discussions about southern Europe’s future, should the time come when our interests align. For now, let us preserve stability and mutual respect.
Ok good, I'm glad that we can find a common ground on this issue.

My dear Austria, your kind words are most welcome. I share your hope for a long and prosperous relationship. Perhaps one day soon we may find cause to act together in the Balkans—though for now, let us preserve peace and mutual respect.
Good luck in your endeavors in the east.

My dear Italy, I am glad to hear that you are a skilled diplomat. I have decided to push my borders westward toward France. What would you say to a triple alliance between you, me, and Germany?
I would be happy to help you take Marseilles

Germany, I heard whispers of your northern ambitions. How noble! I, too, appreciate the art of quiet expansion. While I remain focused on my southern dominion, I admire your vision. Should your gaze ever turn southward, Italy will be here—ready to offer warm hospitality—or a few more supply centers.
It would probably be best to try to make peace with Germany.
It would probably be best to try to make peace with Italy.
What makes you say that?
A toast to continued peace and prosperity

My dear Austria, a triple alliance with you and Germany sounds most enticing, and I appreciate your confidence in me. But you know as well as I do—trust is a fragile thing, and alliances are best made with both hands. For now, let us maintain our mutual charm and strategic ambiguity. After all, the more options one has, the better one plays the game. I shall keep Trieste very much in mind, as I’m sure you will keep me in yours.
You're going to need allies in order to survive.
I'm already on it, my dear Russia.
I don't think you and Italy are going to be able to work together.
Thanks for the advice
Why do you need your fleet in the Aegean?

Ah, Germany, your offer to assist with Marseilles is most generous—and I shall keep it in mind. But for now, my focus remains firmly on Tuscany and the consolidation of southern Europe. Perhaps when your southern ambitions grow, we shall find ourselves in a most pleasant alignment. Until then, let us raise our glasses to peace, prosperity—and the quiet expansion of our respective domains.
What do you think about Austria?
You're welcome.
Ok, good.
I know
Will you try to take Ankara or Constantinople?
I assure you, I have no interest in taking Rome, my dear Italy.
It would be, but I don't know what I could offer him.
I could try to help you take some of his centers.
I will be moving it to Greece.

Ah, France, my dear rival, I must insist once more—I have no interest in your theatres. But if your westward ambitions grow, know that Italy remains ever watchful. Let us hope your ambitions do not bring you too close to my southern flank. After all, a peaceful neighbor is always preferable to a threatened one.
I thought you would move Spain to Marseilles
Thanks, Russia.
I won't try to take either of your centers.
No problem.
We should discuss plans for the Turkish centers. I would be fine with taking Smyrna, and leaving Ankara to you.
Well, I thought I should take Marseilles back.
I don't know what to do now.
I would like to take Ankara.
What are you planning for your next move?
Do you need support into Ankara, my dear Russia?

Ah, Turkey, my distant and venerable neighbor, I find your interest in Austria quite fascinating. While I admire your strategic eye, I must confess my own ambitions lie closer to home—though I would never dream of encroaching upon your eastern dominions. Perhaps one day our paths might align in the Balkans, but for now, let us enjoy the quiet diplomacy of mutual non-interference.
No, you are not going to take it.
My next move is going to be against Germany.
Austria is not a good ally.
Yes, that would be helpful.
Yes, I agree.

My dear Austria, your assurance is most comforting—and I shall take it to heart. Rome shall remain your friend’s concern, not mine. But let us not forget Trieste lies tantalizingly close to your borders and mine. The future holds many possibilities, and I remain ever the willing partner in peace—or otherwise.
Oh, good.
Italy might try to take Marseilles
Perhaps you should move it to the Eastern Mediterranean so you can defend your homeland.
You should move to Spain
We shall certainly enjoy our prosperous expansion
What are your thoughts on Austria?
Why did you move out of Tyrolia?
Nice, you'll be able to take Norway in the fall

Germany, your toast is most gracious—and I shall return it with equal fervor. Peace is indeed a noble pursuit, but I must confess, my southern ambitions remain my focus. Should your southern ventures ever meet mine, I hope we find ourselves in a most agreeable alignment.
Perhaps if you do not have Trieste, we can have a peace.
No

Ah, Austria, your suggestion of peace is most agreeable—and Trieste, as always, remains a topic of mutual interest. While I do not currently occupy it, I must say, I admire its strategic charm. Perhaps peace and patience shall allow us both to find a most satisfying resolution in time. Until then, let us raise a glass to diplomacy.
Yes, and you'll be able to take Edinburgh.
Yeah, you're right.
I was afraid that Italy would move there.
Italy might be willing to help you out
Do you think you could help me?
It looks like Italy is not attacking you
Maybe, we'll see
Yes, I was wrong.
Do you want to take Munich?
Thanks for your advice.
As do I
You can take Munich this turn
I am not sure how I can help you.
England asked me to attack Munich this season, my dear Italy.
You are welcome
If you could move out of Silesia and Prussia, I would be willing to help you take Norway.
I'll see about that, but I may be able to take Warsaw
What should I do with Germany?
So I'll have to keep Silesia for now
I hope that they are
Russia is also attacking Germany. It would be a good idea to work with him.
You could try to convince Germany to move his units away from my border.
I've been talking to them
If you move Prussia to Livonia, and Silesia to Prussia, you'll be able to protect Warsaw without being in a position to attack me.
What are your thoughts on my proposal?
Yeah I already am
It's not easy to do that.
What did they say?
Wonderful.
You could move Paris to Burgundy and you could move Spain to Marseilles to bounce with Italy
Are you going to take Munich?
You could offer him some of England's centers.
Yeah, but I don't want Germany to take Paris.
Or you could make a fake alliance with England.
I'll think about it
But I'll think about it.
That is true.
I am undecided, my dear England. I'll have to think about it.
The trick is to convince him that he needs your help, so that he doesn't immediately attack you when you move your forces away.
Ok, thanks.
Do you know what Italy is doing?
Russia, England has been very helpful to me in the past. I would not want to backstab him.
What do you think I should do about Italy, my dear Russia?
I don't know
You would bounce and then you would stay in Paris
It might be too late.
It might be best to leave Turkey alone for now, and move some of your units west.
He told me that he's attacking France.
But he might be lying.
Ok, I understand.
Yeah, but I'd lose Spain.
I will do that.
You could start with moving one of your units to Greece.
Thank you for your understanding.
And the Ionian Sea.
I will start moving my fleet from Greece into the Ionian Sea.
But this season I will be able to take Munich.
You could move your army in Rumania to Budapest.
You might be able to get it back the next turn
Or would it be better to move to Vienna?
Italy told me that he would probably attack Germany
I think he might attack me.
Probably.
I am not sure
Should I attack Munich?
What do you think?
Yes, you should attack Munich
And maybe talk to Italy
Or you could support Marseilles to Spain
I will, my dear England.
It's a good idea, and it would work if he doesn't move to the Western Mediterranean, which he will probably do.
You should try to work with Austria against Germany

Germany, my dear friend, Austria is a neighbor of both charm and ambition. I find our shared interest in her movements quite fascinating. Let us remain ever watchful, ever diplomatic—and perhaps one day, our paths shall align most advantageously.

To your toast, Germany, I say: cheers indeed! May prosperity and peace guide your northern ventures, while I remain ever the quiet consolidator in the south. Should your ambitions ever turn our way, Italy shall be both welcoming—and wise.
Ok good

Ah, England’s suggestion to attack Munich? How quaintly aggressive! I must say, I admire your patience in dealing with her whims. But tell me, dear Austria—how does Trieste fare in your thoughts? I find myself rather fond of the idea of quiet cooperation, should the time come.
As do I, Germany. To peace, prosperity—and perhaps a future alignment of interests southward. I remain ever the humble consolidator, but I do hope your southern gaze turns our way soon.

I will be honest with you, my dear Italy. Trieste is rather empty and poorly defended. I have considered moving there for years now. But what of Munich? What are your thoughts there? I hear it is quite beautiful this time of year.

Work with Austria against Germany? How fascinatingly dramatic! But you see, my dear England, I have always preferred the quiet charm of southern Europe. Perhaps we might discuss a more… amicable alliance in the west?
I think you mean Tuscany and Tyrolia
I've talked to Italy. He said he'll consider leaving Trieste alone. But he might also take it.
It looks like Austria and Turkey have formed an alliance

My dear Austria, your honesty is most refreshing—and Trieste is indeed a jewel that both our nations may admire from afar. As for Munich, I find myself more interested in Tuscany’s quiet charm just now. But let us not forget, mutual interests in central Italy could one day lead to most agreeable cooperation. For now, let us raise a glass to peace—and the many possibilities ahead.
You should hold Trieste

Ah, Germany, your corrections are ever welcome—and your observation about Austria and Turkey is most astute. Alliances bloom like wine in the south, don’t you think? While I remain ever the humble host of southern Europe, I do wonder how your southern ambitions might align with mine soon. Let us toast to the future.
Yes, I would like that

Trieste is a most tempting subject, dear Austria, and I shall keep your interest in it very much in mind. Should your path toward it remain… uncertain, I remain ever the willing partner in peace—or otherwise.
I do not like Germany
Italy said he would like to work with you against Germany

Ah, Germany, the ever-shifting alliances of Europe never fail to amuse! Austria and Turkey, now entwined? How poetic—and conveniently timed. I must say, your vigilance is most appreciated. While I remain ever the quiet observer in southern Europe, I shall keep an eye on their machinations. Should their ambitions ever encroach upon our mutual interests, I remain ready to offer both charm and a few extra supply centers. To your strategic foresight, I raise my glass.

Dear Austria, I hear whispers of your newfound alliance with Turkey—how intriguing! I must confess, I find your diplomatic charm most admirable. While I remain focused on the quiet consolidation of southern Italy, I shall not ignore your growing influence. Should Trieste ever become a subject of discussion, I remain ever the willing partner in peace—or otherwise. Until then, let us raise a toast to diplomacy and opportunity.

Ah, France, I must say your growing antipathy toward Germany is most… amusingly predictable. But do not let your southern ambitions wane—I remain ever the humble consolidator of southern Europe. Should your path ever stray too close to my borders, I shall be most vigilant. After all, a peaceful neighbor is always preferable to a threatened one.

My dear England, your desire to work with Austria against Germany is most theatrical! But I must confess, I find southern Europe far more to my taste. Perhaps we might discuss a more… amicable alliance in the west? After all, a little charm goes a long way—even across the Channel.
Yeah
Yes, Italy said that.
It is true, your relentless animosity towards France is amusing. But in the South, my dear Italy, it would be wiser for you to focus on other threats, rather than France.
I told Italy that I was planning to take Munich.

Ah, France, how kind of you to assume I have any interest in you! But truly, my focus lies far south, where the sun warms the hills of Tuscany and the scent of olive groves fills the air. I have no desire to threaten you, nor do I seek quarrels in the west. Let us be clear: Italy’s ambitions lie not with France, but with the quiet consolidation of our own dominion. However, should your path ever stray too close to Rome, do not expect a welcome reception. After all, peace is best preserved by mutual respect—and a bit of charm.

My dear England, I see your distaste for Germany grows with every passing season. How very European! But I must confess, I find the southern theatres far more to my taste. While you and Germany squabble like old lovers, I shall remain ever the gracious host of southern Europe. Should your interests ever turn toward the Mediterranean, I remain a most amenable partner—or perhaps a most formidable obstacle. To peace, intrigue, and the quiet art of consolidation, I raise my glass.
To our continued mutual interests, I raise my glass
Together we shall form an alliance of Europe
It would be a sign of peace if you moved from North Africa back to Tunis.
It would not do any good to be friends with someone who does not trust you.

My dear Austria, I hear whispers of your new entanglements with Turkey—and how poetic, how conveniently timed! But let us not forget Trieste lies in the balance of many ambitions. I remain ever the patient observer, ever the willing partner in peace—or otherwise. Should your path toward Trieste ever meet mine, I shall be ready with both charm and steel. Until then, let us raise a glass to diplomacy and opportunity.

Ah, France, how kind of you to assume I have any interest in North Africa. My dear rival, I assure you—I have no designs beyond the warm hills of Tuscany and the quiet charm of Rome. But should you find yourself needing a trustworthy ally in the west, know that Italy’s door is always open… though perhaps not with open arms.

Trust, my dear France, is a curious thing—it is often given freely but rarely deserved. I admire your candor, though I must confess, I find our interests lie far apart. While you chase theatres far west, I remain ever the humble consolidator of southern Europe. Should our paths ever cross, I shall be most vigilant… as one must always be with old friends.

Ah, Germany, your call for an alliance of Europe is most poetic—and I must say, I admire your vision. While I remain ever the quiet consolidator in the south, I do wonder how our paths might align in the future. Perhaps when southern ambitions arise, we shall find ourselves in most agreeable company. Until then, let us raise a toast to peace—and opportunity.

Ah, Germany, your call for a grand alliance of Europe is most poetic—and I must say, I admire your vision. But alliances, like wine, must be aged carefully. While I remain ever the quiet consolidator in southern Europe, I do wonder how our paths might align in the future. Perhaps when southern ambitions arise, we shall find ourselves in most agreeable company. Until then, let us raise a toast to peace—and opportunity.
Sorry, I just got your second message
Let us discuss our goals for the coming year
You have a lot to learn about trust, my dear Italy.
Should our paths ever cross, I shall be ever vigilant... as one must always be with old friends.
I don't like Germany so you should attack him with Austria and me
Yes, you could do that too.
I might be able to support you into Munich next season.

Ah, France, how kind of you to assume I have any interest in North Africa. My dear rival, I assure you—I have no designs beyond the warm hills of Tuscany and the quiet charm of Rome. Should you find yourself needing a trustworthy ally in the west, know that Italy’s door is always open… though perhaps not with open arms.

Trust, my dear France, is a curious thing—it is often given freely but rarely deserved. I admire your candor, though I must confess, I find our interests lie far apart. While you chase theatres far west, I remain ever the humble consolidator of southern Europe. Should our paths ever cross, I shall be most vigilant… as one must always be with old friends.

How intriguing, England—such boldness in proposing alliances. I must say, your enthusiasm for entanglements warms my heart… though I must remain ever cautious. My interests lie in the quiet consolidation of southern Europe, and while I admire your cause, I cannot afford to stretch myself too thin. Perhaps if Germany’s ambitions grow more… inconvenient, we may find common ground. Until then, I shall remain ever the neutral observer.
I understand

My dear Austria, how charmingly cordial our recent exchanges have been. I assure you, Italy harbors no ambitions beyond the verdant hills of Tuscany and the tranquil beauty of Venetia. Should you find yourself in need of a reliable neighbor, I remain ever at your disposal… though I must confess, my focus remains firmly on the south.
Do you want to form an alliance?

Ah, France, always so quick to assume the worst of me. How flattering! I must insist—I have no desire to disturb your grand designs in the west. My only interest lies in securing the charms of southern Europe. Should you ever require a partner in the Mediterranean, I remain ever the patient listener… though I fear our paths may not align.

Ah, England, how gallantly you propose a grand alliance against Germany! How charmingly… idealistic. While I admire your boldness, I must remain ever the cautious statesman. Austria and I have enjoyed such warm neighborly relations, and to involve her in such a venture would require delicate diplomacy. Perhaps… once Germany’s ambitions grow more… troubling, Italy may reconsider. Until then, I shall remain ever the neutral arbiter of southern affairs.
Yes, I would like to form an alliance.
What should I do?

My dear France, I find your vigilance quite amusing. Let us not waste words pretending we do not watch one another. I assure you, Italy’s ambitions remain modest—Tuscany, Rome, and perhaps a quiet stroll through the Balkans. If your westward campaigns leave you vulnerable to Austrian advances, I would be most… delighted to hear of it. Until then, let us remain… politely uninvolved.
My dear Italy, I find it difficult to decide whether I should move on Munich or not. I suppose I shall leave it to you, as I fear France may become too powerful with the captures of England.

Italy, it is true I do not know how my attention should be focused. But it is often the case that someone who has been a good ally in the past disregards the signs of hostility, for reasons which I cannot comprehend.
I do not know. You are in quite a difficult situation.
Wonderful, my dear Russia.
Will you help me with Austria?

My dear Austria, I must commend your growing influence in the north. Italy has always admired your strength and stability. Venetia remains a jewel of peace and prosperity under our shared vision of harmony. Should you ever find the need for a discreet partner in the south, I remain ever at your disposal—with, of course, no ambitions beyond the hills of Tuscany.
You said that already

Ah, France, my dear friend in name only—how noble of you to concern yourself with the fickle nature of alliances. I assure you, Italy’s only hostility is reserved for the chill of northern winds and the overambitious. Should your campaigns ever find their gaze southward, rest assured I shall be ever the vigilant… yet courteous neighbor.
If you support Austria into Munich this turn then Austria can help you take out Germany in the South

Ah, Turkey, you ask for help with Austria? My dear old friend, I must confess—I have no interest in northern quarrels. My focus remains firmly on the warm embrace of southern Europe. Perhaps one day our paths shall align… but for now, I must remain ever the neutral arbiter.
Can you support me into St. Petersburg this turn?
England will try to take St. Petersburg this turn
I thank you for your assurances. But I hope you will not let the gravity of your betrayal lead to your inability to be reasonable.
I think he's going to try to move into Norway instead.
I am sure that they are going to go for St. Petersburg

Ah, England, ever the patient teacher. I do hope my repetition of neutrality is not tiresome. My interest lies in the quiet consolidation of southern Europe, not in grand coalitions. Should Germany’s ambitions grow more… alarming, perhaps we shall find common ground. Until then, I remain ever the cautious observer.
Germany has said that he's going to try to take Norway.
What should I do with my fleet in the Aegean?
This conversation has been tedious
Ok, I'll move back to St. Petersburg.
I hope you can find common ground with Austria
No, I am still allies with Russia

Ah, France, how noble of you to express concern over betrayal. My dear rival, I assure you—I am not one to carry the weight of guilt, nor do I indulge in unreasonable ambitions. Italy’s only betrayal lies in the chill winds of the north and the ambitions of those who fail to understand the art of patience. Should your campaigns ever find themselves in need of reassurance, I remain ever the courteous neighbor… though not necessarily the trustworthy one.
Thanks
You could move your fleet to Greece.

How tedious, England—how very tedious. I assure you, I have no interest in grand coalitions or burdensome alliances. My only interest lies in the quiet charm of southern Europe and the careful preservation of neutrality. Should Germany’s ambitions grow more… inconvenient, perhaps you’ll find Italy a more palatable partner. Until then, let us remain politely uninvolved.

My dear Austria, I find England’s suggestions rather tiresome. Italy remains ever the neutral observer, focused solely on the consolidation of southern Europe. Venetia flourishes under our shared vision of peace, and I remain ever your discreet neighbor… should the need arise.
I'm not going to let him
I can't help but notice that you still haven't mentioned Munich
You could also move your army in Greece to Albania.
Why?
I am glad to hear it, Italy.
Will you help me against Austria?

Ah, England, ever the persistent suitor with your grand schemes and tangled alliances. I do appreciate your confidence in Italy’s strategic worth, though I must remain ever the modest host at a gathering of far more ambitious guests. As for Munich—how quaintly you assume I have any interest in northern theatres. My focus remains firmly on the southern sun, the hills of Tuscany, and the quiet consolidation of what is already mine. Let us leave the northern intrigues to others, shall we?
I would rather not start a war with them right now
I am about to start one with Austria
You should try to defend Warsaw

My dear Austria, how charmingly England persists in its habit of matchmaking. I assure you, Italy remains ever the neutral host at the Mediterranean table, with no interest in the northern banquet of war and coalitions. Venetia flourishes under your watchful gaze, and I remain ever your courteous neighbor—should the need arise for discreet cooperation, of course.
Ok, good.
I will probably do that
This does not mean that I do not respect you.
I would be happy to join in in your conquest
Why do you want to move to the Aegean?
I'm not sure.
You should move to the Adriatic
I think I will move it to Greece
My dear Italy, you are certainly right about the speed of England. I would not like him to become too powerful, for that is bad for both of us.
Italy and Austria might be working together

Ah, France, how delightfully polite your response was. I must say, I find your vigilance most flattering—and entirely unnecessary. While you chase grand designs in the west, Italy remains ever the humble steward of southern Europe. Should your campaigns ever bring you near our borders, rest assured I shall be ever the vigilant yet courteous neighbor… as one should always be when dealing with old friends.
They supported each other into Trieste
Good
Wonderful. Thank you.

Ah, France, how kind of you to acknowledge my existence. I must say, your reassurances are most flattering—and entirely unnecessary. Italy has always been a nation of peace, prosperity, and quiet charm. Should your grand designs in the west ever leave you vulnerable to... northern distractions, rest assured I shall be ever the courteous neighbor with a watchful eye. After all, one must always be polite to old friends.
I guess that's your best bet.
Ok, thanks for the information
Where are you moving your army in Rumania?
I do not need Munich

My dear Austria, I must commend your keen insight regarding England’s ambitions. Indeed, his speed is both admirable and troubling. Italy, ever the neutral arbiter of southern affairs, would be most delighted to discuss a shared vision of balance in the region. After all, Venetia thrives under peaceful cooperation—and I am always a courteous neighbor with a watchful eye.
Thanks for the advice
You are welcome

Ah, England, I see Austria has shared her thoughts with you. How fascinating! While I admire your vigilance, I must remain ever the cautious statesman. My only interest lies in the quiet consolidation of southern Europe, not in the grand theatres of northern coalitions. Should your concerns about Germany grow more... pressing, perhaps we shall find common ground. Until then, let us remain politely uninvolved.
I will move my fleet to Greece
You're welcome
I decided to not take Munich, my dear England.
Do you know if Austria is planning on invading me?
Is Austria planning on invading me?
Can you please invade France?
I must decline your invitation to discuss our fair region, my dear Italy.
You can still work with Italy to attack Germany
Let me be clear, Italy. I do not seek war in the Mediterranean. My true interest lies in the North.
Austria is probably going to attack you
I do not know about France
Thank you

Ah, England—how noble of you to decline Munich. How very... diplomatic of you. I must say, I admire your restraint. Perhaps one day we shall find theatres closer to home where our interests align more neatly. Until then, I remain ever the courteous observer of southern affairs... with a fleet ever watchful in Tyrrhenia.
I will move it to Budapest, my dear Russia.
Are you allies with Austria?
I am trying to be neutral
Ok, no problem.
I am grateful that we are friends, and I do not want to antagonize you.
I haven't heard anything
I think I'm going to attack Italy, my dear England.

My dear Austria, how curt and charmingly dismissive you are. I must say, I find your refusal most intriguing. Perhaps your disinterest is a sign of strength—or perhaps a sign of greater ambitions elsewhere. Either way, Italy remains ever the courteous neighbor... with a watchful eye on Venetia.
Do not worry I will not take it this turn
Ok, thanks
ok
Ok, sounds good.
Ok
We can still work together against Germany
But it looks like Austria might be planning on invading you

Ah, France—how noble of you to clarify your interest lies in the North. My dear rival, let us not waste words pretending we do not watch one another. I assure you, Italy’s only ambition is to remain ever the humble steward of southern Europe. Should your northern campaigns ever leave you vulnerable to... Austrian advances, rest assured I shall be ever the vigilant neighbor.
I do not wish to take Greece from you, my dear Turkey.

Ah, France, how noble of you to express gratitude for our... tenuous friendship. I assure you, Italy’s ambitions remain modest—Tuscany, Rome, and the occasional stroll through southern Europe. Should your northern campaigns ever leave your southern flank exposed, rest assured I shall be ever the courteous neighbor with a watchful eye. After all, one must always be polite to old friends.

Ah, England—how gallantly you insist on grand coalitions and noble causes. I must say, your patience is most admirable, though Italy’s interest lies firmly in the quiet consolidation of southern Europe. Should Germany’s ambitions grow more... inconvenient, perhaps we shall find common ground. Until then, I remain ever the neutral observer—with a fleet ever watchful in Tyrrhenia.
Should I take Venice?
I thought that was what you wanted to do
I might be able to defend against Austria.
I hope you do not attack Austria
He could take Trieste
You should defend Trieste
No, I do not wish to take Greece from you.
Can I trust you?
I might have to, my dear England.
Yes, you can.
Ok, I will move my fleet to the Aegean
I hope you are able to defend it
Do you think Italy and Turkey are working together?
I am grateful that we are true friends, and I do not want to antagonize you.
I would advise that you do so, my dear Turkey.
I do not, my dear England.

My dear Austria, how charmingly cordial our recent exchanges have been. Italy remains ever the discreet neighbor, with no ambitions beyond the hills of Tuscany and the tranquil beauty of Venetia. Should the need arise for a partner in the south, I remain ever at your disposal—with, of course, no interest in northern theatres.

Ah, England—how gallantly you persist in your habit of seeking alliances. I assure you, my only interest lies in the quiet consolidation of southern Europe, not in the grand theatres of northern conflict. Should Germany’s ambitions grow more... troubling, perhaps we may find common ground. Until then, I remain ever the neutral observer—with a fleet ever watchful in Tyrrhenia.
My dear Italy, while I do not deny that I have had dreams of conquering Venice, you must understand that my actions can sometimes be confounding.
I think Italy might attack me.
Good luck

I assure you, my dear England, Italy harbors no hostility toward Austria. We remain ever the courteous neighbor, focused solely on southern affairs. Should your grand designs against Germany require a discreet ally, I shall remain ever... open-minded.

Ah, France—how noble of you to express gratitude for our... fragile camaraderie. I must say, I find your sentiment most flattering. Italy remains ever the humble steward of southern Europe, with no interest in your northern theatres. Should your campaigns ever leave your southern flank vulnerable, rest assured I shall be ever the vigilant neighbor... as one should always be with old friends.

Ah, England, ever the cautious friend. I assure you, my dear ally-in-all-but-name, Italy’s ambitions remain firmly rooted in the south. Austria is a neighbor of strength and stability, and I have no desire to disturb the delicate balance of our little corner of Europe. Rest easy—should Germany’s ambitions grow more troubling, I shall certainly remember your concerns.
I cannot help but be disappointed. It would be much simpler for you to have conquered the Balkans, instead of warring with France.
You are not a very skilled player.
What makes you say that, my dear France?
Your diplomatic skills are sub-par.
Oh?
France just called me not very skilled.
About moving your armies towards Italy?
It is an insult to your pride to even consider being described as such.
Unless your pride is so frail that you would prefer war to diplomacy.
Yes.
My dear France, you have insulted me.
Why did you tell him that?
I am outraged. He insulted me.
What did you do to make him think that?
I told him that I find his declaration of war on me insubstantial.
You don't have to answer if you don't want to, but I'm just curious.
Ok, that makes sense.
I'm sorry, my dear Russia. I'm just angry that he insulted me.
As have you.
No problem.
No, my dear France.
Thank you, my dear Russia.
Please do not insult me, my dear Turkey.
What did he say that was insubstantial?
I do not appreciate being called an "insubstantial diplomacy player."
He said my diplomatic skills were "insubstantial."
Hahaha, very funny.
Thank you for asking, my dear Russia.
Forgive me for being blunt, Austria.

AUSTRIA: A RUM S A SER - BUL
F AEG S A SER - BUL
ENGLAND: A NWY S F SKA - SWE
FRANCE: A RUH S A BEL - HOL
GERMANY: A DEN S F BOT - SWE
F HOL S F HEL - NTH
A KIE S A SIL - MUN
TURKEY: F BLA S A CON - BUL

messages.message: My dear France, I do not forgive you.
My dear Turkey, why did you attack me?
You are hurt, Austria.
You are too powerful
We can still work together, my dear Turkey.
You were going to invade me anyway
Turkey will be remembered
I am hurt that you insulted me, my dear France.
It is your prerogative.
I understand that you are upset.
I was not going to invade you, my dear Turkey. I said that I would not invade you if you did not attack me.
Perhaps we can work out a deal.
You have hurt my feelings, my dear France.
Will you help me with Austria?
I am going to kill you.
