My esteemed Austria, I find your silence most agreeable. Let us continue to preserve harmony in southern Europe while I enjoy the quiet pleasures of Rome and Tuscany. Should the need arise, I shall always be available to discuss matters of mutual interest.
My dear Italy, I thank you for your consideration.

Greece is a name that echoes through the Balkans, but let us not mistake echo for substance. My ambitions lie not in the north, but in the south, where stability and charm are my greatest allies. When the time is right, perhaps we shall discuss shared interests in the Adriatic. Until then, I remain in Rome, watching with keen interest.

My dear Turkey, your suggestions are ever welcome, though I must confess my heart lies more with quiet stability than grand alliances. Austria, you see, has been most generous in her silence, and I find peace in the south most agreeable. But should you ever wish to discuss shared interests in the Adriatic, I remain ever your attentive listener.

My dear Turkey, your suggestion is most intriguing, though I must confess I find Austria a most agreeable neighbor at the moment. Should her ambitions grow too bold, perhaps we shall revisit the matter. Until then, let us focus on preserving peace in the south. Rome is quite lovely this season.

My esteemed Austria, your kindness is ever a pleasure to receive. I must admit, your quiet strength in the north puts me at ease, and I find great comfort in our mutual understanding. Let us continue to cherish this harmony, perhaps over a glass of Italian wine.
My dear Italy, I look forward to it.
But Italy, look at Venice.
My dear Russia, would you be willing to let me move into Ukraine, my dear?

My dear Austria, your kindness is a pleasure I shall always cherish. Harmony in southern Europe is a vision we both admire, and I find great comfort in knowing you stand so firmly in the north. Perhaps one day we shall toast to greater things, though for now, I shall enjoy the wine of peace.

My dear Turkey, Venice is a city of beauty and history, but ambition is a river that flows far beyond its canals. I must confess my heart lies in the south, where the sun is kinder and the air is more agreeable. Let us not trouble ourselves with the north just yet.
My dear Italy, as I will enjoy the wines of southern Europe.
As long as you are willing, my dear, to share with me.
I'm sorry, Austria, I can't let you do that.

Mon cher France, I hear whispers of your growing strength in the north, but here in Italy, we take pride in our southern grace and quiet strength. Should your fleet ever find itself near Rome, perhaps we shall enjoy a peaceful conversation over wine.
I need to keep my army in Moscow there so that Turkey doesn't take it.
It is a little late for that.

My dear Austria, your generosity is as warm as your wines. I would be delighted to share in southern Europe’s pleasures—though I must confess, my own vineyards yield quite the vintage. Let us toast to mutual prosperity and quiet understanding.
Sorry, Turkey, I need my army in Galicia to support my army in Vienna.
I meant to say Trieste.
I will attack Galicia and cut the support.
Let us find the right time, Italy.
Of course, my dear Austria—I am always willing to share. After all, a neighbor as strong and steadfast as yourself is a treasure to southern Europe. Let us continue to enjoy this harmony.
Thank you.

Mon cher France, I assure you, I was merely making polite conversation. But I do hope you will keep your fleets in check near the Tyrrhenian Sea—after all, Rome is quite fond of its wine, and we mustn’t spill too much of it.

Mon cher France, I assure you, my only interest is in the southern coast, where the wine is warm and the company is charming. If your fleets prefer the Tyrrhenian, I shall raise a glass to your good taste—though I must insist we avoid spilling any of it.

Ah, Trieste—a city of beauty and opportunity. But ambition is a river, my dear Turkey, and mine flows southward. Perhaps one day we shall discuss the Adriatic, but for now, let us enjoy the quiet pleasures of diplomacy.
Sorry
My dear Italy, wine is good.
England, you are getting powerful, and it is making me nervous. Can you please make your army in Sweden go away?
My dear Russia, the right time is always when the moment favors Italy. For now, let us admire your grand ambitions from afar, while I tend to my own vineyards and quiet expansion.
That sounds quite fine.
My esteemed Austria, your strength brings comfort to southern Europe, and I find great peace in your quiet diplomacy. Let us continue to enjoy mutual prosperity and the fine wines of our lands.

My dear Turkey, I apologize if my previous words were unclear—perhaps I spoke too softly for your ears. But rest assured, my ambitions flow southward, where the sun is kinder and the wine is warmer. Let us enjoy peace for now.

Mon cher France, wine indeed is good, and Italy’s vineyards are among the finest. But let us also remember that the Tyrrhenian Sea is quite lovely when fleets remain respectful of our southern borders. To your health!
Ok, that is understandable.

My dear Russia, your ambitions are as grand as your armies, and I admire your vision from afar. For now, Italy shall remain content with her own quiet expansion, but I shall keep your words close in case fortune favors us both.
Thank you.
If you let me take Berlin, then I can
My dear Italy, I suppose it is too much to ask that you leave the Gulf of Lyon?
I agree.
I'm sorry.
My dear Italy, I look forward to our continued friendship.
Ok, but keep in mind that you only need four more supply centers.
It is okay, you are a kind and understanding neighbor.
Ok, I will let you have Berlin, but could you please move the army in Sweden away?
Ok
My esteemed Austria, your strength brings great comfort to southern Europe, and I find peace in knowing you stand so firmly in the north. Let us continue to enjoy harmony and mutual prosperity.

Mon cher France, I am most pleased by your agreement—let us continue to enjoy our mutual respect and the quiet pleasures of southern Europe. I assure you, my fleets shall remain as polite as my wine, and your movements shall be met with the utmost charm.

My dear Austria, your kind words are always a pleasure to receive. Let us continue to enjoy the peace of southern Europe, fortified by your strength in the north and my own growing stability in the south.

My dear Russia, four more supply centers is indeed the mark of true Mediterranean dominance. I shall keep your words in mind, though I must confess I prefer to count them in my own time and with my own pace.
You are a most delightful ambassador, Italy.
That is fine, but once you have conquered the Adriatic, it is just a hop skip and a jump to Trieste.

My dear Russia, you speak with the wisdom of a seasoned general—and I, of course, would be most honored to follow in such footsteps. Four more supply centers is indeed a noble goal, though I prefer to sip my wine slowly and savor each conquest. Trieste is a jewel, but even jewels must be admired before claimed. Rest assured, when the moment is right, I shall rise to the occasion with the grace of southern Europe.
My dear Italy, my wife just gave me a wonderful idea.
If you could help me against France, I promise that I would make sure you end the game with as many supply centers as you could possibly get.

Mon cher France, your compliments are most flattering, though I must confess my charm is but a shadow of my true ambitions. I shall continue to sip my wine in Rome, watching the tides of the Tyrrhenian Sea with the utmost interest. Should your fleets ever drift too close to our southern shores, I shall be most... hospitable.
I think France is getting too powerful.

My esteemed Austria, your strength brings me great comfort, and I find peace in knowing you stand so firmly in the north. Should you ever feel the need to discuss matters of mutual interest—or perhaps share a glass of fine Italian wine—I remain ever your attentive neighbor.

My dear Russia, your wife's idea is certainly intriguing, though I must confess I am most comfortable with my own quiet path to expansion. France is indeed growing strong, but I believe the key to true Mediterranean dominance lies in patience and the careful sipping of wine. If you find yourself victorious in your northern ambitions, perhaps we shall speak again of grander alliances. For now, I shall continue to enjoy the southern sunshine.
Italy, my fleets have no intentions of going anywhere close to your southern shores.
What are you planning to do next?

My dear Turkey, France’s growing strength is indeed a matter of concern. But let us not forget the beauty of the Adriatic and the quiet charm of southern Europe. I must confess my ambitions lie not in the north, but in securing the stability of Rome and Venice. Perhaps one day we shall find common ground, though for now, let us watch France from afar.

My esteemed Austria, your strength brings great comfort to southern Europe, and I find peace in knowing you stand so firmly in the north. Should you ever feel the need to discuss matters of mutual interest—or perhaps share a glass of fine Italian wine—I remain ever your attentive neighbor.

My dear Russia, your words are most generous, and I appreciate your confidence in me. France’s rise is indeed troubling, though I find myself more preoccupied with the quiet pleasures of Rome and the consolidation of southern stability. Should the moment arrive when my wine is ready and your plans are set, I shall be most eager to toast to a grand alliance. For now, let us sip slowly and await the tides.
You should move Ionian Sea back to Tunis to defend against France.

Ah, France’s power is a shadow cast over Europe, my dear Turkey. While I admire your vigilance, I must confess my own ambitions flow southward, where the wine is warm and the air is kind. Perhaps one day we shall find common ground in the Adriatic, but for now, let us enjoy the beauty of diplomacy and the quiet strength of our respective positions.
It would be a good idea to move Ionian Sea back to Tunis to defend against France.
I'm not sure. Do you have any suggestions?
Could you move into Venice and support me into Trieste?
Do you think I could take Trieste and Venice?
Austria has many armies there.
It will be very hard to take them.
I know. What if I take Silesia first?
Then what?
Italy, please try to delay your attacks on Austria for as long as you can, because I need his support against Turkey.
Then I would be able to help you take Trieste.
But Russia could take Warsaw.
And they probably won't.
You can always move into Silesia later.
Sorry, I meant could Austria take Warsaw.
Oh
Yes, I can move to Silesia later.
What are you doing with your fleets in the Mediterranean?
Italy told me they wouldn't take it as an act of aggression if I leave the Tyrrhenian Sea.
What did you tell them?
Turkey, if I support move you into Venice, would you help me take Rome?
You need to bounce France out of Rome.
Yes.
I said that I would leave the Tyrrhenian Sea if they did not see it as an act of aggression.
But if they do, you will attack them.
I believe it would be mutually beneficial for us to have a truce
If they help you take Venice, then I will take Rome.
I already have a truce with France
Fine, then
Good luck in your future endeavours
Thanks
Okay.
Do you think it is too risky to attack now?
You have more fleets than them.
But if you think I should wait, then I will wait.
If you wait, they could build a fleet and support hold in Tyrrhenian Sea.
I could move my fleet in the Western Mediterranean to Tunis to stop them from support holding, but that would require leaving Spain or North Africa.
Wait to move into Tyrrhenian Sea.
Okay.
You will need more fleets than them.
My dear Italy, I will endeavor to be ever your attentive neighbor.
Do you have any ideas?
Maybe build another fleet in Marseilles.
Then you can support move into Gulf of Lyon.
Yes, that is what I was thinking.
Then you can support move into Tyrrhenian Sea.
Why?
Because Italy will have to move that fleet in Tyrrhenian Sea.
I admire your tenacity, my dear Turkey.
Yes, but if I leave the Tyrrhenian Sea, they could just support hold.
Are you planning to attack Sweden?
I might do that later.
When?
I don't know yet.
I would watch out for Austria.
Ok, I'll keep that in mind.
And also Sweden.
Yeah.
What are you planning to do?
I'm planning to support Austria in Trieste.
But they could move from Ionian Sea to Tyrrhenian Sea.
Yes, but then I would not be able to take the Gulf of Lyon.
You could support move into Gulf of Lyon.
Yes, but I also could not move the fleet in Spain.
I do not know.
Italy could easily support hold the Gulf of Lyon.
You have to guess what Italy is doing.
It looks like Trieste is probably going to be taken by Italy.
It will most likely not be taken, because I think he plans to try to defend his homeland against you.
Oh.
Sorry.
Do you plan on taking Warsaw?
No, I do not plan to take Warsaw.
Then why did you move a unit to Prussia?
I am not planning to move the unit in Prussia.
ok
Are you seeking to move to Romania?
My dear Italy, I miss you.
I was just defending myself.
My dear Turkey, I understand.
What should we do in the south?

Mon cher France, your assurances are most charming, and I find great comfort in your words. Let us continue to enjoy the peace of southern Europe, fortified by mutual respect and the fine wines of Italy. Should your fleets ever find themselves near Rome, I shall be most... hospitable.
Do you want to team up against Italy?

My dear Austria, how kind of you to write! I hope Vienna is treating you kindly after your recent retreats. I must say, your southern frontier has caught my eye—Trieste is such a beautiful region. Perhaps we could discuss a future where both of us benefit from mutual understanding... though for now, I remain focused on securing the heart of Italy.
What are you going to do with Venice and Tyrolia?

Mon cher France, I must admit, your recent movements have been quite fascinating. I remain ever the neutral observer, but should your ambitions turn southward, I hope you remember the value of diplomacy. A glass of wine and a warm welcome await you in Rome—should you ever find yourself in need of a friend.

Will you help me take Warsaw?
Could you support move me into Trieste?
From Serbia?
I might be able to, I have to think about it

I think it would be beneficial for both of us.
My dear Italy, I can see how Trieste is quite lovely this time of year.

I can attack Greece with Aegean Sea and then Ionian Sea
You are already very powerful
I need more supply centers

My dear Austria, it is always a pleasure to hear from you, and I must say, your affection warms my heart. Your recent movements have not gone unnoticed, my friend. While I remain ever the neutral observer, I must say that the Adriatic is a delicate balance. Should you find yourself needing an understanding ear, I remain open to discussions—over tea and with the utmost discretion.

My dear Turkey, I assure you, my anger is reserved only for those who threaten peace in the Mediterranean. I remain ever the neutral and reasonable power, while others grow impatient and reckless. Perhaps we could discuss how we might both benefit from a more measured approach to our shared interests in the Adriatic.

As for Austria, my dear friend, I am always attentive to the shifting tides of diplomacy. My focus remains on securing southern Italy and maintaining harmony in our corner of Europe. Perhaps one day soon we shall find common cause—should the moment arise.

Mon cher France, your gratitude warms my heart. I shall continue to watch over southern Europe with peace and neutrality in mind. Should your ambitions ever draw you southward, know that Italy stands ready to welcome you—with open arms and a bottle of wine.
Would you be able to help me take Warsaw?
My dear Italy, I was wondering if you would like to help me take Warsaw?
I could help you defend Trieste if necessary.

My dear Austria, I hope Vienna is treating you kindly. I remain ever the interested observer of your northern affairs, though I must confess my own ambitions lie closer to home. Still, I would be delighted to discuss any shared concerns—or opportunities—should you ever feel inclined to share them.
My dear Italy, I will be happy to discuss any matters of mutual concern with you.

Mon cher France, what a kind offer, though I must confess my ambitions lie closer to home—southern Europe has many pressing matters to attend to. That said, should your path ever lead southward, I shall be most eager to lend a hand—or perhaps even a glass of wine.
My dear Italy, I understand your importance in Trieste.
But I do not want to fight a war on two fronts
I am sorry, but I cannot help you take Warsaw
Yes, I will help you take Warsaw.
I do not know if you want to do this, but Austria is planning to attack Venice with Trieste.

As for Trieste, my dear France, I remain ever the neutral observer. That said, if your interests lie northward, perhaps we might find common cause—though I must remain focused on securing Rome and Tuscany for now.

Would you rather start a war with me than with Russia?

My dear Austria, I am flattered by your willingness to discuss matters of mutual concern. Trieste is indeed a beautiful region, and I admire your strength in the north. Perhaps one day we might find ourselves aligned in shared interests—though for now, I remain ever the patient and charming neighbor.

Moving an army from Piedmont to Venice would be a most precarious endeavor, as we do not hold Piedmont. That said, I appreciate your concern for Venetian stability. Perhaps we might find ways to deter Austrian aggression together—though I remain ever the neutral power, content with the beauty of southern Italy.

I am trying to take St. Petersburg

No

Just kidding, I am just nervous that you are so powerful

You support move Aegean Sea into Greece.

Sorry.
